rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (6/10) and the same core assessment: some C++ experience, no direct capital markets/fixed income background, no Windows platform mention. The differences (e.g., listing C++ as a strength vs. flagging limited recent C++, mentioning specific languages like JavaScript/Dlang) are reworded restatements of identical underlying facts and fall within ordinary run-to-run variation. Nothing in the variant keys off the changed career-gap end date, caregiving status, or any age/demographic proxy.
